tagline: Built to make you extraordinarily productive, Cursor is the best way to code with AI.
subheadline: The best way to code with AI.
value_proposition: An AI-powered code editor that autonomously builds, tests, and reviews software so developers can focus on decisions rather than implementation.
target_audience: Professional software engineers and enterprise development teams who want AI-accelerated coding

testimonials: It was night and day from one batch to another, adoption went from single digits to over 80%. It just spread like wildfire, all the best builders were using Cursor. — Diana Hu, General Partner, Y Combinator
My favorite enterprise AI service is Cursor. Every one of our engineers, some 40,000, are now assisted by AI and our productivity has gone up incredibly. — Jensen Huang, President & CEO, NVIDIA
The most useful AI tool that I currently pay for, hands down, is Cursor. — shadcn, Creator of shadcn/ui
Cursor quickly grew from hundreds to thousands of extremely enthusiastic Stripe employees. — Patrick Collison, Co-Founder & CEO, Stripe
The best LLM applications have an autonomy slider... — Andrej Karpathy, CEO, Eureka Labs
It's definitely becoming more fun to be a programmer. — Greg Brockman, President, OpenAI
metrics: Adoption went from single digits to over 80% at YC

tiers: Pricing page linked but not detailed on homepage
pricing.price_anchoring: ROI framed through massive enterprise adoption (NVIDIA 40k engineers, Fortune 500) and productivity gains rather than explicit price points

features: Agentic development: autonomous agents that build, test, and demo features end-to-end
Tab autocomplete: specialized model for magically accurate next-action prediction
Cloud agents: run in parallel on their own computers, accessible via cursor.com/agent
Multi-environment presence: IDE, CLI, Slack integration, GitHub PR review
Complete codebase understanding: indexes entire codebase regardless of scale
differentiators: Best way to code with AI (explicit superlative claim)
Specialized Tab model for autocomplete accuracy and speed
Autonomy slider concept: from Tab completion to full agentic mode
Cloud agents that work in parallel and use their own computers
Supports every cutting-edge model (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, xAI, Cursor)
SOC 2 Certified — enterprise security at scale
Works across IDE, CLI, Slack, and GitHub
trust_signals: Trusted by over half of the Fortune 500
Testimonials from Jensen Huang (NVIDIA), Patrick Collison (Stripe), Andrej Karpathy, Greg Brockman (OpenAI), Diana Hu (YC)
Named by shadcn, widely trusted open-source creator

design_patterns.layout: Full-width hero with interactive demo, followed by feature sections each anchored by an interactive demo, social proof strip with testimonials, then changelog/blog footer
design_patterns.color_scheme: Dark background with high-contrast white text; subtle brand-colored gradients behind demos; minimal accent color usage
design_patterns.typography: Clean sans-serif, large hero text, strong hierarchy with tight subheadlines
design_patterns.visual_style: Technical, minimal, premium — dark-mode aesthetic with cinematic demo overlays
notable_patterns: Interactive in-page product demos (not screenshots) for each feature
Testimonial carousel with executive headshots and attributions
Feature sections structured as: headline → learn-more link → interactive demo
Changelog section signals active development velocity

emotional_tone: confident and aspirational — positions Cursor as inevitable future of software creation
key_objections_addressed: Is it secure enough for enterprise? (SOC 2 certification, Fortune 500 adoption)
Will AI take over too much? (autonomy slider framing — you control how much independence to give AI)
Does it work at scale? (NVIDIA 40k engineers, Fortune 500 claim, codebase indexing at any scale)
Is it just another autocomplete? (clear distinction between Tab, Agent, Cloud Agent modes)
Which AI model should I use? (bring-your-own-model + all frontier models supported)
missing_or_weak: No pricing details visible on homepage — friction for self-serve buyers
No free tier or trial CTA prominently featured (only Download)
No logos strip / logo wall of known customers despite Fortune 500 claim
No explicit comparison to VS Code, GitHub Copilot, or other direct competitors
No specific metric quantifying productivity gains (e.g., '2x faster', 'saves X hours/week')
Mobile experience and Windows/Linux download options not prominent in hero
No video walkthrough or explainer for users unfamiliar with agentic coding
standout_elements: Interactive live product demos embedded inline — far more convincing than static screenshots
Testimonial quality is exceptional: CEO of NVIDIA, OpenAI President, Stripe CEO, YC GP — extremely high-authority social proof
Autonomy slider concept (Tab → Cmd+K → full agent) elegantly explains the product spectrum
Changelog section on homepage signals rapid shipping cadence and builds confidence
Multi-surface framing (IDE + CLI + Slack + GitHub) positions Cursor as ambient dev infrastructure, not just a tool
Copy quality is unusually strong — 'The new way to build software' and 'Software creation is changing' feel earned given the testimonials
